framework.name: RFC 8693 act claims are unvalidated
framework.description: The draft explicitly confirms that nested `act` claims in RFC 8693 are informational only — they can be forged, omitted, or fabricated with no cryptographic binding. This means every current OAuth 2.1 token exchange implementation claiming multi-hop delegation is running on trust, not verification.
framework.raw_quote: prior actors remain informational only and token exchange does not define how a delegation path is preserved and validated

source.title: Cryptographically Verifiable Actor Chain for OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange
